name: json-canvas
description: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.
capabilities: Create new JSON Canvas files with proper structure
Add text, file, link, and group nodes with correct attributes
Define edges connecting nodes with labels and colors
Generate valid hexadecimal IDs for nodes and edges
Build mind maps, flowcharts, and visual diagrams
Validate canvas JSON against JSON Canvas Spec 1.0
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill that provides templates and examples for creating JSON Canvas files. All static findings are false positives triggered by JSON code examples in markdown documentation. No executable code, network calls, or cryptographic operations present. Skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Obsidian JSON Canvas files require precise JSON structure. This skill provides templates and examples to create valid canvas files with nodes, edges, groups, and connections.
limitations: Does not interact with Obsidian application directly
Cannot read existing canvas files from filesystem
Does not support custom node types beyond text, file, link, group
No automatic layout or positioning algorithms

useCases.title: Create Mind Maps
useCases.description: Build visual mind maps for project planning, note organization, or brainstorming sessions with connected nodes

useCases.title: Design Flowcharts
useCases.description: Create flowcharts and process diagrams with decision nodes, directional edges, and color-coded elements

useCases.title: Organize Research
useCases.description: Connect PDF references, notes, and external links in a visual research canvas for literature review

promptTemplates.title: Simple Text Canvas
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a JSON Canvas file with 3 text nodes: a title at the top, and two child nodes below it connected to the title with edges labeled 'Branch A' and 'Branch B'. Use reasonable x, y coordinates and sizes.

promptTemplates.title: Research Reference Canvas
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a JSON Canvas file with a central text node containing 'Research Topic', connected to 3 file nodes pointing to 'Paper1.pdf', 'Paper2.pdf', 'Paper3.pdf', and one link node pointing to 'https://example.com'. Add edges with labels showing relationships.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Step Process
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a flowchart canvas with 5 nodes: Start, Step 1, Decision (Yes/No), Step 2, End. Connect them with directional edges. Use colors to distinguish decision points.

promptTemplates.title: Grouped Organization
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a JSON Canvas with 2 group nodes: 'Group A' containing 2 text nodes, and 'Group B' containing 2 text nodes. Add edges connecting nodes across groups.

bestPractices: Always include unique hexadecimal IDs for every node and edge
Use color attributes to visually distinguish node types
Order nodes by z-index (first in array = bottom layer)
Define edge labels to clarify relationships between nodes
antiPatterns: Forgetting to include the 'nodes' or 'edges' array wrapper
Using duplicate IDs across multiple nodes or edges
Creating self-referencing edges that connect a node to itself

faq.question: What is JSON Canvas?
faq.answer: JSON Canvas is an open file format for infinite canvas data used in Obsidian. Files use .canvas extension and contain valid JSON with nodes and edges arrays.

faq.question: How do I generate unique IDs?
faq.answer: Use 16-character hexadecimal strings. Obsidian generates random IDs, but any unique string works for custom canvases.

faq.question: Can I add images to canvas nodes?
faq.answer: Yes, use file nodes with the 'file' attribute pointing to image paths. The 'subpath' attribute can link to specific sections.

faq.question: What node types are supported?
faq.answer: Four types: text (Markdown content), file (references), link (external URLs), and group (visual containers).

faq.question: How do I control visual layering?
faq.answer: Node order in the array determines z-index. Earlier nodes appear below later nodes. Reorder the array to change layering.

faq.question: Can I create colored edges?
faq.answer: Yes, add a 'color' attribute to edge objects using the same color system as nodes (0-8 range).
